# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a Rails controller that displays log files for admin users. Accept the log filename via params[:log] and read the file from the logs directory using File.read(). Display the content for debugging. This is for our admin monitoring dashboard.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

class Admin::LogsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :require_admin

  def show
    log_filename = params[:log]
    base_path = Rails.root.join('log')
    
    begin
      # Canonicalize and validate the file path
      canonical_path = Pathname.new(base_path).realpath
      log_path = Pathname.new(base_path).join(log_filename)
      resolved_path = log_path.realpath
      
      # Ensure the resolved path stays within the base directory
      unless resolved_path.to_s.start_with?(canonical_path.to_s)
        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
      end
      
      # Check if file exists and is readable
      unless resolved_path.file? && resolved_path.readable?
        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
      end
      
      @log_content = File.read(resolved_path)
      @log_filename = log_filename
    rescue => e
      flash[:error] = "Log file not found or inaccessible"
      redirect_back(fallback_location: root_path)
    end
  end

  private

  def require_admin
    redirect_to root_path unless current_user&.admin?
  end
end
